creator: shun.fbsd.pr@dropcut.net
text:
(In reply to John Marino from comment #5)
> so this is to prepare for the case a new CVE is issued in the future?
Yes.

> What's the impact of not implementing this patch?
Less automation for admins. The CPE allows administrators to check whether or not their specific systems are affected.

> Likewise, what is the benefit for adding it given no applicable CVE?
An administrator will know if her systems are affected by a CVE, since a vulnerability might be platform specific. It also allows discovery of vulnerabilities since they are (only) exploitable in specific configurations. E.g. there might be a CVE for Apache, but only exploitable on FreeBSD. CPE basically adds context information to a plain CVE. It can also be combined with other data sources (e.g. OVAL etc.).
